# Mailbox-array feature matrix

The array is built per **(FPGA, design)** target: pick the core + fabric + memory
features the design needs, drop what it doesn't. Don't ship one monolithic core â€”
select a point in this matrix, generate it, build the bitstream.

Measured on the ZCU104 (xczu7ev, 8Ã—8 = 64 cores, 200 MHz) unless noted.

## Axes

### Core (per-core RISC-V) â€” generated by `fpga/vexriscv/gen_core_variants.sh`
| axis | options | when / cost |
|---|---|---|
| **shifter** | `barrel` \| `light` | `barrel` = single-cycle shifts â†’ **4.5Ã—** on shift/rotate-heavy designs (SHA, xorshift), +~2.5% LUT. `light` = 1 bit/cycle, smaller â€” only if the design barely shifts. |
| **muldiv** | `on` \| `off` | `on` = rv32im Mul+Div, **~4 DSP + LUT/core**. `off` = rv32i, **0 DSP** â€” drop it unless the design multiplies/divides. (Today's accel-C is `rv32i`, so `off` is usually right.) |
| **cfu** | `on` \| `off` | `on` = Custom Function Unit port (`ldx_cfu.v`) for design-specific hot-op acceleration. `off` = smaller core. |

Smallest core (`light:off:off`) â‰ˆ 3,512 lines vs â‰ˆ 5,100 for `barrel:on:on`.
Generate: `./gen_core_variants.sh barrel:off:on` â†’ `core_lib/VexRiscv_barrel_nomul_cfu.v`,
then point `create_8x8_project.tcl`'s `set rtl` core file at it.

### Fabric (inter-core network) â€” `mb_array_soc` params in `mb_array_top.v`
| option | param | when / cost |
|---|---|---|
| flat router | `USE_MESH=0` | any-to-any, O(NÂ²) â€” small arrays only. |
| **NN mesh (SW relay)** | `USE_MESH=1` | nearest-neighbor, cores relay distant traffic in software. No link BRAM. Hung/busy cores BLOCK transit; only tile 0 reaches egress. |
| **HW XY router** | `USE_HWROUTER=1` | per-tile hardware routing. **Any-to-any, no relay code; transit past hung cores; egress from any tile.** Cost: +~12% LUT and **~36% RAMB18 at 8Ã—8** (one FIFO per N/S/E/W link = `4Â·NÂ·(Nâˆ’1)` RAMB18). 8Ã—8 closes 200 MHz (WNS +0.080 ns). |

### Memory (per-core program/data) â€” `MEM_WORDS` + `ram_style`
| option | when / cost |
|---|---|
| **URAM** (`ram_style="ultra"`, default) | private code+data â†’ URAM, **frees ALL dual-port BRAM** for the fabric. 1 URAM/core â†’ caps ~96 cores. |
| BRAM | per-core dpram in BRAM (4 RAMB36 at 16 KB) â€” use to push past the 96-URAM cap (LUT-bound ~128) or on parts with little URAM. |
| `MEM_WORDS` | 1024 / 2048 / 4096. Size to the program. SHA accel-C (`-O2`) needs ~2.5 Kword â†’ 4096 (16 KB). |
| **bank-switching** | per-cycle double-buffer of the signal region (read active / write next, parity-flip at the barrier â€” **no data movement**). HW always supports it (`addr_mode`/`region_base`/`cycle_parity` in `ldx_soc_mailbox`); selecting it **doubles the signal-region memory** (bump `MEM_WORDS`) and the codegen/worker emits banked access. **Measured (bank/mb_bank.c, N=256 stencil): 1.5Ã— vs naive memcpy** (6158â†’4115 cyc/sim-cycle â€” eliminates the ~33% spent copying state back). Approaches **~2Ã— for copy-dominated** designs (pipelines, FIFOs, line buffers, regfiles), less for compute-heavy. The on-array HW addr_mode makes the swap free in the address path, so â‰¥ that. The key per-cycle feature for PARTITIONED designs with a large cross-core signal state â€” not exercised by the single-core SHA/DUT benchmarks. |

## Throughput levers (all in, measured)
`-O2` workers (Ã—2.7), 1-cycle fetch (Ã—1.14), barrel shifter (Ã—4.5) â†’ **235 â†’ 18 cyc/eval (â‰ˆ13Ã—)**:
**~10 M evals/s per core, ~637 M aggregate (8Ã—8) = 2.47Ã— a single x86 thread** â€” for *independent* parallel work. Partitioned-design (banked, cross-core) throughput is TBD.

## Selecting for a target
1. **Design barely shifts and never multiplies?** `light:off:*`, smaller core â†’ more cores.
   **Crypto / datapath / shift-heavy?** `barrel`, and add `cfu` if there's a hot op worth a custom instruction.
2. **One big design partitioned across cores?** `USE_HWROUTER=1` (cross-core comms) + **bank-switching** (per-cycle signal exchange) + size `MEM_WORDS` for 2 banks.
   **Many independent sims?** `USE_MESH=1` is enough (or even no routing); skip banking.
3. **Core count vs the part:** URAM caps ~96/chip (1/core); mix in BRAM-backed cores to reach the LUT limit (~128 on xczu7ev). Bigger parts (VU13P, Stratix 10/D5005) scale ~linearly to ~1,000 cores.
4. **Drop unused blocks:** `muldiv=off` whenever the design has no mul/div (frees DSP + LUT â†’ more cores).
